name: A Goblin's First Scrap
summary: The Goblin Elder wants to see if you can fight. Find him in the settlement to begin.
intro: Ah — a fresh goblin, still smelling of the spawning pit! Good. Before you're any use, you must learn to scrap. Head EAST to the training yard and batter one of the rats. Click the ground to walk, click a rat to hit it.
outro: Ha! Not bad for a first swing. You'll do. Here — a few coins to start you off, and my blessing. The settlement is yours to explore; folk here have work for a goblin with initiative. Look for the '!' markers.

steps.say: Click the ground to send Gork walking. The yard is just east of me.
There's the rat. Left-click it to attack — Gork will walk up and swing on his own.

steps.text: Pick up the Bones it dropped
steps.say: It dropped some bones. Walk over them to pick them up — that's how you loot.

steps.name: Goblin Elder
steps.say: Now bring those bones back to me, back west in the settlement.

name: First Pickaxe
summary: The General Store Keeper needs copper from the quarry.
intro: You've got arms on you — how about you use 'em? There's copper up in the Grubpit Quarry, NORTH of town. Take a pickaxe, bring me five copper ore. Simple.
outro: Proper ore, that. Knew you had it in you. Here's your cut — and remember, anything you gather, you can sell. Try the Grand Exchange by the fountain.

steps.text: Travel north to the Grubpit Quarry
steps.say: Head north up the road. Watch for cave bugs — you can fight or run past them.
Click the copper rocks with a pickaxe equipped or in your pack.

steps.name: General Store Keeper
steps.text: Bring the ore back to the Store Keeper
steps.say: Good haul — now carry it back to me in the settlement.

name: Fish for the Chief
summary: The Wharf Fishmonger is short on shrimp for the Chief's supper.
intro: Chief wants shrimp and the Chief being hungry is EVERYONE'S problem, understand? Grab a small net and pull three shrimp from the water here on the Wharf. Go on.
outro: That'll keep the Chief off our backs. Cook 'em on a fire or range and they'll even heal you in a fight. Here's your pay, little fisher.

steps.say: Click the fishing spots along the Wharf with a small net to catch shrimp.

steps.name: Fishmonger
steps.text: Hand the shrimp to the Fishmonger
steps.say: Bring those shrimp back to me here on the Wharf.

name: Goblin Needs Pointy Stick
summary: The Weapon Shop Keeper in the Forge Ward thinks you need a real blade.
intro: That twig you're waving? Embarrassing. A proper goblin carries a Bronze Dagger. Buy one from me, smith one at the anvil, or take one off something — I don't care how. Just come back armed.
outro: NOW you look like a warrior. Bronze today, iron tomorrow. Keep at the smithing and you'll out-fight the lot of them. Here — a little something for listening.

steps.say: Buy one from my shop, smith one from a bronze bar at the anvil, or loot one.

steps.name: Weapon Shop Keeper
steps.text: Show the dagger to the Weapon Keeper
steps.say: Bring it here and let me see it, back in the Forge Ward.

name: Cabbage for Cowards
summary: The Greengate Grocer swears cabbage makes a goblin brave.
intro: Cabbage! Cabbage makes a goblin BRAVE — that's science, that is. Get down to the farmlands SOUTH of the gate and bring me back three good heads.
outro: Look at these beauties. You're braver already, I can tell. Don't argue. Take your coins and eat your greens.

steps.say: Follow the south road out of Greengate to the farm plots.
Pick cabbages from the farm patches.

steps.name: Grocer
steps.text: Bring the cabbage to the Grocer
steps.say: Carry them back to my stall at Greengate.

name: Rats in the Storehouse
summary: The General Store Keeper has a rat problem — and needs a goblin who can fight.
intro: Right, you can swing a blade now — prove it. My storehouse is overrun. Clear out FIVE of the rats in the training yard and I'll make it worth your while. Off you go.
outro: Five rats, gone. You're handy in a scrap, I'll give you that. Here's your pay — and if you want a real challenge, the world beyond the gates is full of it.

steps.say: The yard's east of town — thin them out.

steps.name: General Store Keeper
steps.text: Report back to the Store Keeper
steps.say: Come tell me when the storehouse is clear.

name: The Hollow Idol
summary: The Goblin Elder has felt something stir beneath the old training yard. He needs a brave goblin to face it.
intro: You've proven you can fight. Good — because I need you for something the others are too frightened for. There is an old idol beneath the training yard, older than the warren itself. Lately it… whispers. Take it three bone offerings, and whatever crawls out — put it down.
outro: So. The Hollow Idol is silent at last, and you walk away wearing its crown. The whispers named you, goblin — I heard them. Keep the Fang close. I suspect this was only the beginning.

introCutscene.say: You hear it too, don't you? The hum beneath the yard, every night since you arrived.
Go. Silence the idol before it wakes the whole warren.

outroCutscene.say: …at last… a goblin worthy of the crown…

steps.text: Seek the idol beneath the old training yard
steps.say: The Elder points east, past the training posts. The ground hums as you approach.
Bones for the idol — the rats in the yard will provide. Lay three at its feet.
The idol splits open with a crack of old stone — and something climbs out!

steps.name: Goblin Elder
steps.say: It's done. Carry the crown back to the Elder in the settlement.

name: Bridge Over Dumb Water
summary: The Timber Row Foreman wants the West Bridge rebuilt — and a squatter cleared off it.
intro: That river's been cutting us off from the willow lands for a season, and now some bandit's set up on the old crossing. Bring me timber and nails, then go clear the bridge yourself. Ten logs, five oak, three bronze nails.
outro: A bridge AND one less bandit. The willow lands are open again — good work, goblin. This is more than pocket change; you've earned it.

steps.say: Chop the trees around town with a hatchet.
Oak grows in Chopper's Hollow to the west — you'll need Woodcutting 15 for it.
Hammer nails from a bronze bar at the anvil.
The crossing is west of town, at the river's edge.
There's the squatter. Send him packing.

steps.name: Timber Row Foreman
steps.say: Come tell me the bridge is ours again.

name: The Grubpit Problem
summary: The Miner Camp Boss can't work the quarry — it's crawling with bugs.
intro: Can't get a lick of work done in that quarry — cave bugs everywhere and imps nicking the ore. You look like you can swing. Clear 'em out: eight bugs, two imps. Then the copper's ours again.
outro: Quarry's quiet for the first time in weeks. The lads can mine again thanks to you. Take this — miners look after those who look after them.

steps.say: The quarry's in the hills — head there and get to work.
Squash the bugs infesting the tunnels.
The imps are tougher — mind yourself.

steps.name: Miner Camp Boss
steps.text: Report to the Miner Camp Boss
steps.say: Come back to the camp when the quarry's clear.

name: A Proper Goblin Axe
summary: The Weapon Shop Keeper dares you to forge your own iron battle-axe.
intro: Bronze was for the fledgling you were. Time for iron. But I'll not hand it to you — SMELT three iron bars and FORGE the battle-axe yourself. That's the only way it means anything.
outro: You made that. With your own hands and your own fire. That's a real goblin's weapon. Wear it proud — you've earned every swing.

steps.say: Iron ore plus the furnace, at Smithing 15. Watch for failed smelts early on.
Take the bars to the anvil and hammer out the axe.

steps.name: Weapon Shop Keeper
steps.text: Show the axe to the Weapon Keeper
steps.say: Bring it here so I can admire your handiwork.

name: The Farmer's Bad Smell
summary: Something is rotten in the farmlands — pests, and a smell nobody will name.
intro: Crops are crawling with bug swarms and there's a stink over the whole field I won't describe to you. Clear four swarms and bring me five onions — Grocer swears the onions help with the smell. They don't. Do it anyway.
outro: Fields are clearing and I've onions to spare. The smell? Still there. But the crops'll live, and that's down to you. Here's your wage, pest-hunter.

steps.say: Back down the south road to the plots.
The swarms move fast — corner them among the rows.
Pull onions from the farm patches while you're there.

steps.name: Farmhand
steps.text: Bring it all back to the Farmhand
steps.say: Carry the onions up to the shed at Greengate.

name: Grublake Fish Thieves
summary: Someone is raiding the Grublake nets. The Fishmonger wants them caught.
intro: Been losing fish off the Grublake nets every night — crazed fisher-goblins, gone feral. Head EAST to the lake, catch six of 'em in the act, and land three honest trout to prove the water's still good.
outro: Six thieves and a fine string of trout. The nets are safe and the Chief eats well. You've come a long way from netting shrimp on my Wharf, goblin. Well earned.

steps.say: Take the east road past the Oakwoods to the lake.
They lurk by the water — deal with them.
Fish the lake spots for trout while you're here.

steps.name: Fishmonger
steps.say: Bring the trout back to my Wharf.
